title: Network Segmentation and Firewall Review
summary: Assess whether firewall rules, remote admin access, DMZ controls, and exception reviews are preventing unnecessary lateral movement.
objective: Assess whether network segmentation and firewall rule governance are designed and operating effectively for critical internal zones and remote administration paths.
scope: Segmentation between user, server, and DMZ zones

controls.name: Approved Firewall Rule Changes
controls.description: Firewall rules should be approved before implementation and reviewed periodically for necessity.

controls.name: Restricted Remote Administration
controls.description: Remote administration should be limited to approved jump hosts or management networks.

controls.name: Zone Segmentation Enforcement
controls.description: User, server, and DMZ traffic should be separated according to the network design standard.

controls.name: Temporary Access Exception Review
controls.description: Temporary network access exceptions should have expiry dates and periodic review.

evidence.title: Firewall Rule Export
evidence.content: The firewall export includes an inbound rule allowing broad remote access from a vendor subnet to the finance server zone, with no expiry date or linked approval ticket.

evidence.title: Change Request for Temporary Access
evidence.content: A temporary access request for patch weekend notes that the rule should be reviewed after the maintenance window, but the follow-up section is blank.

evidence.title: Network Segmentation Diagram
evidence.content: The segmentation diagram shows a jump host between the admin and server zones, but the live firewall export indicates a direct exception path from the user subnet to one application server.

evidence.title: Security Review Memo
evidence.content: Security review notes that the temporary vendor subnet exception was intended only for migration weekend and should have been removed once testing completed.

evidence.title: Network Design Standard
evidence.content: The standard requires approved firewall changes, remote admin access only through managed jump hosts, strict zone separation, and time-bound exceptions for temporary access.

evidence.title: Intrusion Scan Summary
evidence.content: The recent scan summary notes that east-west traffic between the user VLAN and an application server was observed, but the analyst marked it as expected pending a network change window.

issues.title: Broad Firewall Rule Without Approval Trail
issues.description: A remote access firewall rule remained active without clear approval or expiry evidence.
issues.recommendation: Require ticketed approval and automatic expiry for broad temporary firewall access.

issues.title: Direct Access Bypassed Network Segmentation
issues.description: A live exception path allowed user subnet traffic into a server zone outside the normal design.
issues.recommendation: Block non-standard network paths unless approved compensating controls are documented.

issues.title: Temporary Access Exception Not Removed
issues.description: An access exception created for a migration remained in place after the stated maintenance window.
issues.recommendation: Track temporary network exceptions to expiry and review them after the change window closes.

inbox.subject: Kickoff: Network Segmentation and Firewall Review
inbox.preview: Test whether network paths are actually controlled or just documented as controlled.
inbox.body: Focus on how firewall rules are approved, whether remote admin access is really limited to managed paths, and whether temporary exceptions are removed when they should be.

inbox.subject: Firewall artifacts attached
inbox.preview: Sharing the current export, network diagram, and migration notes.
inbox.body: The environment has a few legacy exceptions from prior migrations. Some of them were meant to be temporary, although a few may still be listed until the next clean-up cycle.

interviewPrompts.question: Why is the vendor subnet still allowed into the finance server zone?
interviewPrompts.answer: That path was opened for migration weekend and was supposed to be removed afterward. I do not think the cleanup ticket was ever closed out.

interviewPrompts.question: Is remote admin traffic restricted to jump hosts?
interviewPrompts.answer: Mostly yes, but one exception path still exists for a legacy application server. It was meant to be temporary, yet the live export suggests it is still active.

interviewPrompts.question: Was the network exception reviewed after migration?
interviewPrompts.answer: We expected a review after the change window, but the follow-up note was vague and I cannot see evidence that the exception was formally removed.

interviewPrompts.question: Are the segmentation alerts expected traffic or a control break?
interviewPrompts.answer: Some east-west traffic is expected during patch windows, but this particular path was flagged because it does not match the normal architecture.

closeout.managementMessage: The network control environment is documented, but temporary exceptions and direct paths are weakening the design. Firewall governance needs tighter approval and cleanup discipline to keep segmentation meaningful.
closeout.auditorReflection: Network reviews usually hide their real risk in exceptions. The key question is whether the live rule set still matches the approved architecture.
